name: matminer (Machine Learning Feature Extraction for Materials)
pricing_notes: Open source (modified BSD); Anubhav Jain / LBNL

auth.notes: Local for featurization; Materials Project API key for data retrieval

performance.notes: NumPy/pymatgen-backed; featurization scales with structure complexity

agent_readiness.notes: Materials ML featurization and dataset utilities. from matminer.featurizers.structure import SineCoulombMatrix, RadialDistributionFunction. from matminer.featurizers.composition import ElementProperty, Meredig. sc = SineCoulombMatrix(). df['features'] = sc.featurize_many(df['structure']). from matminer.datasets import load_dataset. df = load_dataset('dielectric_constant'). from matminer.data_retrieval.retrieve_MP import MPDataRetrieval. MPDataRetrieval(api_key).get_dataframe({'elements': {'$all': ['Fe', 'O']}}, properties=['structure', 'band_gap']). Composition features: Magpie, Meredig.

gotchas: Materials Project API key required for data retrieval
Some featurizers are slow (SineCoulombMatrix for large supercells)
featurize_many can fail silently on some structures — check for NaN
best_when: Machine learning on materials data (composition or structure features)
Featurizing crystal structures for ML force fields and property prediction
Accessing curated materials datasets (band gaps, elastic, dielectric)
Materials Project database querying with ML pipeline
avoid_when: When DScribe's symmetry-adapted features are preferred
summary: matminer provides materials ML featurization and dataset access. SineCoulombMatrix().featurize_many(structures). ElementProperty, Meredig composition features. load_dataset('dielectric_constant'). MPDataRetrieval for Materials Project access. BSD by LBNL/Anubhav Jain. Best for composition/structure featurization in materials ML pipelines. Requires pymatgen. Some featurizers slow for large structures.
